1.1: And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon Yonan, son of Matti, saying:
1.2: ”Arise, go to Nineva the great city and proclaim against it, because their evil has come up before me”
1.3: And Yonan stood to flee to Tarshish from before LORD JEHOVAH and came down to Yoppa, and he found a ship that went into Tarshish, and he gave payment and came down to it to enter with them to Tarshish to flee from before LORD JEHOVAH
1.4: And LORD JEHOVAH cast a great wind into the sea, and there was a great storm in the sea, and the ship being turned was to be broken to pieces
1.5: And the sailors were afraid and they cried out, each man to his god, and they cast away the vessels of the ship into the sea that they would be clear of them, but Yonan had gone down by himself to the bottom of the ship and fell asleep
1.6: And the High Sailor came to him and said to him: ’why are you sleeping? Arise, call to your God! Perhaps God will save us and we will not be destroyed!’
1.7: And they said, each man to companion, ’come, we will cast lots and we will know because of whom this evil has come upon us, and they cast lots and the lot of Yonan came up
1.8: And they said to him: ’Show us why this evil has come upon us! What is your work and from where are you, and what is your land and from which people are you?’
1.9: Yonan said to them: ‘I am a Hebrew and I worship LORD JEHOVAH, the God of Heaven who made the sea and the dry land’
1.10: And those men were afraid with great fear and they said to him: ’what have you done?’, because those men knew that he fled from before LORD JEHOVAH
1.11: And when he showed them, they were saying to him: ‘what shall we do to you and the sea will be still for us, because, behold, the sea is moving and disturbed against us?’
1.12: Yonan said to them: ‘pick me up and throw me into the sea, and the sea will be still for you, for I know that this great storm was upon you because of me!’
1.13: And those men fought to return to dry land, and they were not able, because the sea was moving and was violent against them
1.14: And they called to LORD JEHOVAH and they said: ’Oh, LORD JEHOVAH, we will not be destroyed with the life of this man, and do not account innocent blood against us, because you are LORD JEHOVAH, and as you please you do!’
1.15: And they picked up Yonan and they threw him into the sea, and the sea was calmed from its storms
1.16: And those men feared with great fear before LORD JEHOVAH and they sacrificed sacrifices to LORD JEHOVAH and they vowed vows
1.17: And LORD JEHOVAH prepared a great fish and it swallowed Yonan, and Yonan was in the intestines of the fish for three days and three nights

2.1: And Yonan prayed before LORD JEHOVAH God from the intestines of the fish
2.2: And he said: “I cried to LORD JEHOVAH in my distress and he answered me; from the belly of Sheol I cried and you heard my voice!
2.3: And you cast me into the depths into the heart of the sea and the floods surrounded me; all your storms and your waves passed over me
2.4: But I said: ‘I am removed far from before your eyes, even now I continue to see your holy temple
2.5: The waters surrounded me unto the soul and the depths surrounded me, and my head was shut in at the bottom of the sea
2.6: And to the bottom of the mountains I have come down, and Earth had locked its bolts in front of my face for eternity, and you brought up my life from destruction, LORD JEHOVAH my God!
2.7: When my soul was troubled, I remembered LORD JEHOVAH, and my prayer entered before you to your holy temple
2.8: All who observe empty religions forsake your mercy
2.9: But I shall offer to you with a voice of thanksgiving and anything that I have vowed; I shall accomplish payment to LORD JEHOVAH!
2.10: And LORD JEHOVAH commanded the fish and it vomited Yonan onto dry land

3.1: And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was on Yonan a second time saying:
3.2: Arise, go to Nineva the great city and preach upon it the preaching that I say to you
3.3: And Yonan stood and he went on to Nineva, according to the word of LORD JEHOVAH, and Nineva the city was great to God, a three days’ journey
3.4: And Yonan began to enter Nineva, a journey of one day, and he preached and said: ”From now to forty days, Nineva is overthrown!”
3.5: And the people of Nineva believed in God and they cut a fast and they put on sackcloth, from their Princes and unto their little ones
3.6: And the word arrived to the King of Nineva, and he arose from his throne and he removed his crown from himself and he wore sackcloth and sat down on ashes
3.7: And he proclaimed and said to the Ninevites from the commandment of the King and of his Princes: ”Children of man and beasts and oxen and flocks shall not taste a thing and they shall not feed, neither shall they drink water
3.8: But they shall be covered in sackcloth, children of man and beast, and they shall call God in groaning and a man shall turn from his way of evil and from the plunder that is in his hands
3.9: Who knows if God turns and shows mercy upon us and turns from us the heat of his wrath that we will not be destroyed!
3.10: And God saw their works, that they repented from their evil ways, and he turned the heat of his wrath from them, and he did not destroy them

4.1: And Yonan grieved with great grief and it was very distressing to him
4.2: And he prayed before LORD JEHOVAH and said: Oh, LORD JEHOVAH, was not this my word when I was in my land, and because of this I arose early, I myself fled to Tarshish, for I had known that you, God, are compassionate and hovering and your Spirit is patient, and your kindness abundant, and you change evil!
4.3: Therefore, my Lord, take my life from me, for it is better for me to die than to live!”
4.4: And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: ”You are very grieved for yourself!”
4.5: And Yonan went outside of the city and sat down by himself at the east of the city, and he made himself a booth there, and he sat down under it in the shade to see what happened to the city
4.6: And LORD JEHOVAH commanded a shoot of a gourd and it sprouted and it came up over Yonan and there was shade on his head, and it relieved him from his calamity, and Yonan rejoiced for the shoot of the gourd with great joy
4.7: And the next day LORD JEHOVAH God commanded a worm at the rising of dawn and it struck the shoot of the gourd and it lopped it off
4.8: And when the sun was going down, LORD JEHOVAH God commanded the hot wind and it dried up the gourd and the sun beat on the head of Yonan and he was weary and he requested death for his soul, and he said: ”It has come into your hands, LORD JEHOVAH, to take my soul from me, for I am no better than my fathers!”
4.9: And LORD JEHOVAH God said to Yonan: ”You are very grieved for yourself because of the shoot of the gourd.” And Yonan said: ”I am very grieved for myself, unto death!”
4.10: LORD JEHOVAH said to him: ”You had pity on the shoot of a gourd for which you did not labor, neither did you raise it up, which sprang up in a night, and in a night it dried up
4.11: But shall I not show pity on Nineva, the great city that has in it more than one hundred twenty thousand children of men who do not know between their right hand and their left hand, and a multitude of cattle?
